name: Ceremonial Magic
description: Ceremonial Magic, also known as High Magic or Ritual Magic, is a complex and multifaceted system of magical practice rooted in Western esoteric traditions. Its origins can be traced back to a synthesis of Hermeticism, Neoplatonism, Jewish Kabbalah, and Greco-Roman magic. It aims to invoke or evoke spirits, angels, or deities to achieve specific goals, often involving personal transformation, spiritual enlightenment, or influencing events in the material world. Ceremonial Magic emphasizes elaborate rituals, precise timing, and the use of consecrated tools and symbolic language. It involves a structured approach, often incorporating elements of astrology, numerology, and geomancy. Practitioners typically spend years studying and training in order to master the intricate techniques and understand the underlying principles. The cultural significance of Ceremonial Magic lies in its role as a pathway to spiritual understanding and its influence on Western occultism and esoteric thought. It offers a framework for exploring the hidden dimensions of reality and harnessing the power of the unseen forces that govern the universe. Different systems exist, such as Enochian magic, Thelemic magic, and Golden Dawn-inspired magic, each with its own unique features and methodologies.
subtitle: Invoking Spirits, Shaping Reality

keyMyths.title: The Fall of Lucifer
keyMyths.description: Lucifer's rebellion against God and subsequent fall from grace is often interpreted as a symbol of the magician's own struggle for self-mastery and ascension.

keyMyths.title: The Emerald Tablet
keyMyths.description: Attributed to Hermes Trismegistus, the Emerald Tablet outlines the principle of "As above, so below," a cornerstone of Hermetic magic and Ceremonial Magic.

sources.author: Eliphas Levi
sources.description: A foundational text in Ceremonial Magic, outlining its principles and practices.
sources.text: A foundational text in Ceremonial Magic, outlining its principles and practices.
